stories.headline: Alaska is the field-test range for the XPRIZE Wildfire Autonomous Response track finals
stories.notes: Strong verified structural spine from xprize.org (competition, tracks, roster, prize structure, ACUASI) plus FAA confirmation of ACUASI as a designated test site and ASU News for fire-season stakes. FINALIST DISCREPANCY RESOLVED: xprize.org lists FIVE finalists in the Autonomous Wildfire Response track (Anduril, Data Blanket, Dryad, FireSwarm Solutions, Wildfire Quest); the Space-Based Detection track is separate with 10 finalists. Dryad's own release saying it stood among only three finalists CONTRADICTS the official 5-team roster and must NOT go on-slide. The autonomous detection-and-suppression demonstration is a Dryad self-report (businesswire), echoed by mustreadalaska which credits 'the company announced'; it is NOT independently verified and may only appear with explicit attribution.

stories.headline: Anchorage-HQ ReconCraft LLC wins ~$25M Navy contract for autonomous low-profile vessels
stories.notes: Fully verified against the primary DoD contracts page (war.gov). CAUTION: the announcement describes the vessels only as 'autonomous low-profile vessels.' The scout's 'semi-submersible / reduced radar-acoustic-visual signature / recon + mine-warfare / stealth' descriptors are NOT on the page and were dropped. Alaska tie is corporate HQ only; work is performed in Clackamas, Oregon.

stories.headline: FY2027 NDAA Alaska provisions per Sen. Sullivan's office
stories.notes: SINGLE-SOURCE: every figure comes from Sen. Sullivan's own press release (06.23.26). Verified against that page verbatim, but not independently corroborated, and these are AUTHORIZATION provisions (not enacted appropriations). Use only with 'according to Sen. Sullivan's office' / 'would authorize' framing. Fallback tier only.

claims.text: XPRIZE Wildfire is a four-year, $11 million global competition to accelerate solutions that end destructive wildfires.
claims.source_title: Meet the Future of Autonomous Wildfire Response: XPRIZE Wildfire Announces Finalist Teams Advancing in $11M Competition
claims.quote_verbatim: Total competition purse: $11 million
claims.guardrail: Corroborated by businesswire release (Dryad) and mustreadalaska, both stating 'four-year, $11 million global competition.'

claims.text: The Autonomous Wildfire Response track has five finalist teams: Anduril (USA), Data Blanket (USA), Dryad (Germany), FireSwarm Solutions (Canada), and Wildfire Quest (USA).
claims.source_title: XPRIZE Wildfire Announces Finalist Teams
claims.quote_verbatim: Autonomous Wildfire Response Track (5 finalists): Anduril (USA); Data Blanket (USA); Dryad (Germany); FireSwarm Solutions (Canada); Wildfire Quest (USA)
claims.guardrail: This is the authoritative roster. Do NOT say 'final three' on-slide. A separate Space-Based Wildfire Detection track has 10 finalists and competes in Australia; keep the two tracks distinct.

claims.text: The Autonomous track finals field-test across a 1,000 square kilometer test zone in Alaska.
claims.source_title: XPRIZE Wildfire Announces Finalist Teams
claims.quote_verbatim: Test zone: 1,000 km2
claims.guardrail: Also stated verbatim in the Dryad businesswire release ('a vast 1,000 square kilometer test area in Alaska') and mustreadalaska.

claims.text: The Alaska testing partner is the Alaska Center for Unmanned Aircraft Systems Integration (ACUASI) at the University of Alaska Fairbanks Geophysical Institute.
claims.source_title: XPRIZE Wildfire Announces Finalist Teams
claims.quote_verbatim: Testing partner: Alaska Center for Unmanned Aircraft Systems Integration (ACUASI) at University of Alaska Fairbanks Geophysical Institute
claims.guardrail: ACUASI is the on-the-ground partner; UAF runs it.

claims.text: ACUASI is one of the FAA-designated UAS test sites.
claims.source_title: FAA UAS Test Sites
claims.quote_verbatim: Alaska Center for Unmanned Aircraft Systems Integration, AK
claims.guardrail: FAA lists ACUASI by name as the sole Alaska test site among its designated UAS test sites. The 'Pan-Pacific UAS Test Range Complex' name did not appear on the fetched FAA list; use 'FAA-designated UAS test site' only.

claims.text: The winning team in each XPRIZE Wildfire track receives $3.5 million.
claims.source_title: XPRIZE Wildfire Announces Finalist Teams
claims.quote_verbatim: Final prizes per track: $3.5 million to winning team in each track

claims.text: A $1 million bonus prize sponsored by Lockheed Martin is available to eligible Autonomous teams demonstrating superior detection.
claims.source_title: XPRIZE Wildfire Announces Finalist Teams
claims.quote_verbatim: Bonus prize: $1 million (Lockheed Martin sponsor) for eligible Autonomous teams demonstrating superior detection

claims.text: A $750,000 semifinal purse was distributed equally among the five Autonomous finalist teams.
claims.source_title: XPRIZE Wildfire Announces Finalist Teams
claims.quote_verbatim: Semifinal awards: $750,000 distributed equally among five Autonomous Finalist teams

claims.text: The XPRIZE Wildfire winner is expected to be announced in September 2026.
claims.source_title: Dryad Networks Reaches XPRIZE Wildfire Finals Milestone
claims.quote_verbatim: Expected announcement in September 2026
claims.guardrail: Use 'expected'; xprize.org states finals completion in Q3 2026, which is consistent with a September date but not a hard-confirmed day.

claims.text: The finalist teams were narrowed from an original field of nearly 300 teams worldwide.
claims.source_title: Dryad Networks Reaches XPRIZE Wildfire Finals Milestone
claims.quote_verbatim: selected from an original field of nearly 300 teams
claims.guardrail: Use 'nearly 300'; figure appears in the Dryad release and mustreadalaska, not on the xprize page fetched.

claims.text: XPRIZE Wildfire program director Andrea Santy said wildfires are becoming more frequent, intense, unpredictable and destructive.
claims.attribution: Andrea Santy, XPRIZE Wildfire Program Director
claims.source_title: XPRIZE Wildfire Announces Finalist Teams
claims.quote_verbatim: Globally, wildfires are becoming more frequent, more intense, more unpredictable, and more destructive.
claims.guardrail: Attribute to Andrea Santy by name.

claims.text: Dryad Networks says its Silvanet sensors and Silvaguard drone detected a small wildfire within minutes of ignition and autonomously suppressed it with no human on scene during Alaska field trials.
claims.source_title: Dryad Networks Reaches XPRIZE Wildfire Finals Milestone
claims.quote_verbatim: detected a small wildfire within minutes of ignition and triggered an autonomous response ... located and attacked the fire without human involvement
claims.guardrail: MUST be attributed to Dryad on-slide ('Dryad says' / 'according to Dryad'). This is a company self-report echoed by mustreadalaska (which credits 'the company announced'); it is NOT independently verified. Do not state it as fact.

claims.text: Dryad says its Silvaguard drone can deliver up to 100 liters of fire suppressant directly onto an ignition point.
claims.source_title: Dryad Networks Reaches XPRIZE Wildfire Finals Milestone
claims.quote_verbatim: up to 100 liters of fire suppressant directly onto the ignition point
claims.guardrail: Company spec, self-reported; attribute to Dryad.

claims.text: Dryad says more than 30,000 Silvanet sensors have been manufactured and deployed globally and that Silvanet has been deployed by more than 50 customers worldwide.
claims.source_title: Dryad Networks Reaches XPRIZE Wildfire Finals Milestone
claims.quote_verbatim: More than 30,000 sensors have been manufactured and deployed ... globally. ... deployed by more than 50 customers worldwide.
claims.guardrail: Self-reported deployment figures; attribute to Dryad.

claims.text: Dryad CEO Carsten Brinkschulte said the competition validated that ultra-early detection combined with rapid autonomous suppression can fundamentally change how society fights wildfires.
claims.source_title: Dryad Networks Reaches XPRIZE Wildfire Finals Milestone
claims.quote_verbatim: The competition validated that ultra-early detection combined with rapid autonomous suppression can fundamentally change how society fights wildfires.
claims.guardrail: Attribute to Brinkschulte. If used on-slide, present only this single sentence.

claims.text: Alaska has experienced three of its four largest fire seasons in the last 25 years.
claims.source_title: Using AI to help predict wildfires in Alaska (ASU News)
claims.quote_verbatim: Alaska ... has experienced three of its four largest fire seasons in the last 25 years.
claims.guardrail: Background context (article dated 2025-10-28, outside the run window); label as background.

claims.text: In some years, such as 2022, Alaska accounted for nearly half of the total US acreage burned by wildfires.
claims.source_title: Using AI to help predict wildfires in Alaska (ASU News)
claims.quote_verbatim: In some years, such as 2022, Alaska accounted for nearly half of the total U.S. acreage burned by wildfires.
claims.guardrail: Say 'nearly half' and cite 2022 as the example year (wording is 'in some years, such as 2022'). Do not generalize to every year. Background context (2025-10-28).

claims.text: ReconCraft LLC of Anchorage, Alaska was awarded a $24,960,408 firm-fixed-price contract for autonomous low-profile vessels.
claims.source_title: Contracts for July 8, 2026 (Department of War)
claims.quote_verbatim: ReconCraft LLC,* Anchorage, Alaska, is awarded a $24,960,408 firm-fixed-price contract for autonomous low-profile vessels.
claims.guardrail: Scope on the page is only 'autonomous low-profile vessels.' Do NOT add 'semi-submersible / stealth / reduced signature / mine-warfare' descriptors; they are not in the source.

claims.text: The ReconCraft contract number is N00024-26-C-6324, a Naval Sea Systems Command award.
claims.source_title: Contracts for July 8, 2026 (Department of War)
claims.guardrail: NAVSEA (Washington, D.C.) is named as the contracting activity on the page.

claims.text: Work on the ReconCraft contract is performed in Clackamas, Oregon and is expected to be completed by November 2028.
claims.source_title: Contracts for July 8, 2026 (Department of War)
claims.quote_verbatim: Work will be performed in Clackamas, Oregon, and is expected to be completed by November 2028.
claims.guardrail: Alaska tie is corporate HQ only; the build happens in Oregon. Be honest about this on-slide.

claims.text: The ReconCraft contract was not competitively procured, per FAR 19.805-1(b)(2).
claims.source_title: Contracts for July 8, 2026 (Department of War)
claims.quote_verbatim: This contract was not competitively procured in accordance with Federal Acquisition Regulation 19.805-1(b)(2).
claims.guardrail: FAR 19.805-1(b)(2) is an 8(a)/small-business sole-source authority; frame factually, not as favoritism.

claims.text: The full $24,960,408 was obligated at award from FY2026 accelerated procurement and fielding of innovative technologies (Navy) funds.
claims.source_title: Contracts for July 8, 2026 (Department of War)
claims.quote_verbatim: Fiscal 2026 appropriation account accelerated procurement and fielding of innovative technologies (Navy) funds in the amount of $24,960,408 will be obligated at the time of the award

claims.text: According to Sen. Sullivan's office, the FY2027 NDAA authorizes a historic $2.6 billion in military construction and projects specifically for Alaska.
claims.attribution: Sen. Dan Sullivan press release (single source)
claims.source_title: Sullivan Secures 56 Provisions, Historic $2.6 Billion for Alaska in FY2027 Defense Authorization
claims.quote_verbatim: authorizes a historic $2.6 billion in military construction and projects specifically for Alaska
claims.guardrail: Single-source (Senator's own release). Frame as 'authorizes' / 'according to Sen. Sullivan's office', not enacted spending.

claims.text: The FY2027 NDAA includes 56 provisions and amendments authored by Sen. Sullivan.
claims.attribution: Sen. Dan Sullivan press release (single source)
claims.source_title: Sullivan Secures 56 Provisions
claims.quote_verbatim: includes 56 provision and amendments authored by Sen. Sullivan
claims.guardrail: Single-source; attribute to Sullivan.

claims.text: The bill authorizes $15 million for resilient autonomous sensing in the Arctic.
claims.attribution: Sen. Dan Sullivan press release (single source)
claims.source_title: Sullivan Secures 56 Provisions for Alaska in FY2027 NDAA
claims.quote_verbatim: $15 million for resilient autonomous sensing in the Arctic
claims.guardrail: Single-source; 'would authorize'.

claims.text: The bill authorizes $10 million for autonomy within the Golden Dome missile defense system.
claims.attribution: Sen. Dan Sullivan press release (single source)
claims.source_title: Sullivan Secures 56 Provisions for Alaska in FY2027 NDAA
claims.quote_verbatim: $10 million for autonomy within the Golden Dome missile defense system
claims.guardrail: Single-source; 'would authorize'.

claims.text: The bill mandates rigorous grid impact assessments before executing or renewing any Enhanced Use Lease (EUL), reviewing energy demands and commercial grid stability.
claims.attribution: Sen. Dan Sullivan press release (single source)
claims.source_title: Sullivan Secures 56 Provisions for Alaska in FY2027 NDAA
claims.quote_verbatim: mandates that DOW conduct rigorous grid impact assessments, requiring a formalized review of energy demands and commercial grid stability, before executing or renewing any Enhanced Use Lease (EUL)
claims.guardrail: Single-source; this is the data-center/grid gate tie-in. Frame as an NDAA requirement, not yet in force.

claims.text: The bill would establish an Analytics and Simulation Laboratory within the Ted Stevens Center for Arctic Security Studies.
claims.attribution: Sen. Dan Sullivan press release (single source)
claims.source_title: Sullivan Secures 56 Provisions for Alaska in FY2027 NDAA
claims.quote_verbatim: Establish an Analytics and Simulation Laboratory within the Ted Stevens Center for Arctic Security Studies
claims.guardrail: Single-source; 'would establish'.

dropped.claim: Dryad stood among only three finalists in the Autonomous track.
dropped.reason: CONTRADICTS the authoritative xprize.org roster of FIVE Autonomous finalists. c2 (five finalists) is the verified fact.

dropped.claim: ReconCraft vessels are semi-submersible / stealth with reduced radar, acoustic and visual signatures for reconnaissance and mine-warfare support.
dropped.reason: Not present on the war.gov contract page, which describes only 'autonomous low-profile vessels.'

dropped.claim: XPRIZE Wildfire has a PG&E sponsor providing more than $1 million in support.
dropped.reason: Not found on the fetched xprize.org finalists page. Only Lockheed Martin's $1M bonus was confirmed.

dropped.claim: ACUASI operates as the 'Pan-Pacific UAS Test Range Complex.'
dropped.reason: That name did not appear on the fetched FAA test-sites list. FAA-designated status is verified (c5); the label is not.
